revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Gyōda Futagoyama Kofun]][[ja:二子山古墳 (行田市)]]
{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Gyōda Futagoyama Kofun | native_name = 二子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Gyoda Futagoyama Tumulus In Spring 1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Gyōda Futagoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Gyōda Futagoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.126261|N|139.479058|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Gyoda|qid=Q860659|1=Gyōda}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of ={{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|qid=Q2213580|1=Sakitama Kofun Cluster}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Gyōda Futagoyama Kofun'''|二子山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Gyōda Futagoyama Kofun has a total length (including moat) of 132.2 meters, and is thus the largest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster (and in {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|qid=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province|1=Musashi Province}}). It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with most other tumuli at this site, it has a double rectangular moat. The current inner moat is filled with water, but it was originally a dry moat. Another unusual feature is a protrusion extending from the western side of the tumulus, near the joint between the rectangular and circular portions. Called a "Tsukuridashi", this is a feature which also appears in the {{ill|Inariyama Kofun|en|Inariyama Kofun|qid=Q3198272|lt=Inariyama Kofun|1=Inariyama Kofun}}, [[Teppōyama Kofun]] and {{ill|Shogunyama Kofun|ja|将軍山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogunyama Kofun|1=Shōgun'yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11458633}} in the Sakitama Kofun Group, but is rarely found in other locations.
The internal structure of the tumulus and its [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are unknown, as it has not been [[Archaeological excavation|excavated]]s; however, from a [[:en:Ground penetrating radar|Ground penetrating radar]] curved in 2017, it appears that there is a horizontal-type stone chamber in the east side of the posterior circular portion. Portions of the tumulus were endanger of collapse due to erosion from water in the inner moat, and repair work was conducted from 2013 to 2018. Many cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' over one meter in size have been excavated from the moat, along with some {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery, from which the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the first half of the 6th century AD.
;Overall length: 132.2 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 67.0 meter diameter x 11.7 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 83.2 meters wide x 13.7 meters high
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|qid=Q6573282|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{template-inline|Futagoyama Kofun (Gyoda)}} *[https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/15/04/12/map/futago.html Gyoda city home page] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210602215906/https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/15/04/12/map/futago.html |date=2021-06-02 }} {{in lang|ja}} *[https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/埼玉古墳群紹介/二子山古墳 Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}
